components.title: Claude Code
components.description: The Claude Code agentic CLI — the default agent runtime (installed on every agent).

components.title: Claude Code Router
components.description: Local CCR proxy on 127.0.0.1:3456 that routes Claude Code to a configured provider via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL in ~/.agent-env. Requires Claude Code.

components.title: Google Chrome
components.description: Chrome browser (required by the SideButton extension and Claude-native browsing).

components.title: SideButton MCP server
components.description: MCP server on :9876. Unlocks dispatch from the portal and agent capabilities (roles). Without it the agent is RDP/manual only and not dispatchable.

components.title: SideButton Chrome extension
components.description: Force-installs the SideButton extension via Chrome managed policy and waits for the browser_connected handshake.

components.title: Knowledge packs
components.description: Universal 'agents' ops pack (dispatch ops workflows) + the per-account knowledge-pack registry and its update timer.

components.title: Docker
components.description: Docker engine (enables container builds and Testcontainers-based test suites). Adds the agent user to the docker group.

components.title: PostgreSQL client
components.description: psql command-line client for DB inspection and migration scripts.

components.title: OpenVPN client
components.description: OpenVPN client + the sb-vpn-connect helper. Drop a .ovpn profile at /etc/sidebutton/config/openvpn/ (portal Files hub or manual SSH) and it auto-connects on boot via openvpn-client@; removing it tears the tunnel down. Break-glass: sudo sb-vpn-connect <profile.ovpn>.

config_files.label: OpenVPN profile (.ovpn)
config_files.description: Client profile incl. inline private key; applied via sb-vpn-connect (net_gateway-pinned so the heartbeat stays off-tunnel).

components.title: WireGuard client
components.description: WireGuard client (wireguard-tools) + the sb-wg-connect helper. Drop a .conf profile at /etc/sidebutton/config/wireguard/ (portal Files hub or manual SSH) and it auto-connects on boot via wg-quick@; removing it tears the tunnel down. Split-tunnel; no heartbeat carve-outs. Break-glass: sudo sb-wg-connect <profile.conf> [name].

config_files.label: WireGuard profile (.conf)
config_files.description: Client tunnel config incl. inline private key (+ optional preshared key); applied via sb-wg-connect. One keypair per device — prefer agent scope for concurrent agents.

components.title: RDP client (FreeRDP)
components.description: FreeRDP (xfreerdp) + the sb-rdp-connect helper. Opens an outbound RDP session to a customer host (e.g. an on-prem Windows ERP with no API) and renders it as a fixed-geometry window on the agent desktop (:10) — driveable by computer-use, visible in LiveDesktop, auto-reconnecting on drop. Drop the target host + credentials at /etc/sidebutton/rdp.env (portal Files hub or manual SSH, out-of-band, never in cloud-init) and sb-rdp starts automatically.

config_files.label: RDP connection (rdp.env)
config_files.description: RDP_HOST/RDP_USER/RDP_PASS (+ optional RDP_DOMAIN/RDP_GEOMETRY/RDP_EXTRA). sb-rdp-connect idle-waits on this exact file; presence enables sb-rdp.
